exercises.title: Detect the single-agent ceiling
exercises.prompt: A single agent hits three walls: context overflow, mixed expertise, and the sequential bottleneck. Write a TypeScript function `diagnoseCeiling(task)` where task is `{ docs: number, tokensPerDoc: number, domains: string[], independentSubtasks: number }` and return which ceilings are hit given a 200k-token context window. Run it on a task with 8 docs × 40k tokens across 3 domains with 5 independent subtasks.
exercises.hint: Context overflow: docs*tokensPerDoc > 200_000. Mixed expertise: domains.length > 1. Sequential bottleneck: independentSubtasks > 1 (work that could parallelize is forced serial in one agent). Return the list of triggered ceilings.

exercises.title: Pick the orchestration pattern from task structure
exercises.prompt: Four patterns — pipeline, parallel fan-out, supervisor, hierarchical — fit different task shapes. Write `selectPattern(task)` that chooses based on: whether stages depend on each other (pipeline), whether subtasks are independent and uniform (fan-out), whether subtasks need dynamic planning/synthesis (supervisor), or whether the work is a deep org-chart of sub-teams (hierarchical). Show it classifying four example tasks correctly.
exercises.hint: Decision tree: if stages are ordered+dependent -> pipeline; else if subtasks independent and a single planner can enumerate them -> parallel fan-out; else if subtasks need a planner to decompose dynamically and synthesize -> supervisor; if subtasks themselves decompose into sub-teams -> hierarchical. Encode the signals as booleans.

exercises.title: Quantify the multi-agent token tax
exercises.prompt: Multi-agent systems win on capability but cost more tokens — Anthropic reported their research system used ~15× the tokens of single-agent. Build `compareCost(task)` that estimates tokens for single-agent (everything in one growing context) vs supervisor (lead + N workers with fresh windows) and returns both the token ratio and whether multi-agent is justified (capability gain must clear the cost). Model the single-agent context as quadratic in steps (re-reading accumulated context) and show why fresh worker windows break that.
exercises.hint: Single-agent: context grows each step, so total tokens ≈ sum over steps of accumulated context ≈ O(steps^2 * tokensPerStep). Supervisor: each worker handles a slice in a fresh window (linear), lead pays for summaries only. Compute ratio; justified if estimatedQualityGain > some threshold tied to the cost multiple.
